name: Coria and Torrejoncillo Loop
name_original: Coria a Torrejoncillo – circular por Rincón del Obispo
description: This moderate 27.9-mile mountain biking route near Coria offers a scenic circular ride through Extremadura's varied landscapes.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Coria offers a diverse landscape for riders. The region features a mix of historical urban areas, such as the town of Coria with its ancient Roman walls and cathedral, alongside surrounding natural terrain. Routes often traverse varied countryside, including areas that may feature river crossings, as indicated by landmarks like the Iron Bridge of Coria. The elevation changes across the mountain bike trails Coria Cáceres provide options for different skill levels, from moderate to more challenging ascents. Best mountain bike trails around Coria The most popular mountain bike trail is Coria and Torrejoncillo Loop, a 27.9 miles (44.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects two towns, offering a varied journey through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Calzadilla Loop from Coria, a difficult 29.1 miles (46.9 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation changes and diverse terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Coria Castle and Cathedral – Iron Bridge of Coria loop from Coria, a 24.6 miles (39.5 km) trail leading through historical landmarks and natural river crossings, often completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes. Mountain biking around Coria is defined by historical sites, river areas, and varied countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Coria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 14 reviews. More than 130 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Coria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Coria?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Coria, offering a diverse range of experiences for riders. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Coria?
A: Mountain bike trails around Coria feature varied countryside, often including river areas and historical urban sections. You can expect a mix of surfaces as routes traverse different landscapes, from natural paths to areas near historical landmarks like the Iron Bridge of Coria.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Coria?
A: Yes, Coria offers options for various skill levels. While most routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy trail available. For a moderate option that connects two towns and offers a varied journey, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022446285/coria-and-torrejoncillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coria and Torrejoncillo Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in Coria?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Coria has several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022484934/calzadilla-loop-from-coria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calzadilla Loop from Coria&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult path featuring significant elevation changes and diverse terrain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Coria – Coria Castle and Cathedral loop from Coria&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass by historical landmarks or points of interest?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Coria integrate the region's rich history. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20132640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coria Castle and Cathedral – Iron Bridge of Coria loop from Coria&lt;/a&gt; leads through historical landmarks and natural river crossings. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31872321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coria Historic Center – Coria Castle and Cathedral loop from Coria&lt;/a&gt;, which passes through the town's historic core.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or scenic natural features along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region offers natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorrerón Waterfall and Stone Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural attraction that can be incorporated into or visited near some routes, providing a scenic stop during your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Coria?
A: While specific seasonal conditions can vary, generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for mountain biking in this region. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler, wetter conditions. Always check local weather forecasts and trail conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there loop trails available for mountain biking in Coria?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Coria are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022446285/coria-and-torrejoncillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coria and Torrejoncillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022484934/calzadilla-loop-from-coria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calzadilla Loop from Coria&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy about the trails in Coria?
A: The mountain bike trails in Coria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 14 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscape, which combines historical sites with varied countryside and river areas, offering options for different ability levels.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Coria?
A: While many outdoor areas are dog-friendly, specific rules can vary by trail or protected area. It's always best to check local regulations for each specific route you plan to ride to ensure dogs are permitted and if any restrictions (like leashing) apply.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trailheads in Coria?
A: Parking availability can vary depending on the specific trailhead. In and around the town of Coria, you will likely find public parking options. For routes starting from specific villages or natural areas, it's advisable to check maps or local information for designated parking spots.
tips: The Iron Bridge in Coria is a beautiful civil work of metal architecture and nineteenth-century modernist design. It was built by the Asturian engineers D. Salvador and D. Próspero Ardura. The bridge stands over the Alagón River, connecting both banks in the City of Coria. Its total length is 178.30 meters, and its deck has a width of 7.78 meters, with a two-way road flanked by two sidewalks.
Torrejoncillo is a small and charming typical Extremadura town with a nice square, small narrow streets and beautiful festivals.

The Medieval Bridge of Coria is a historical structure that is located next to the main façade of the Monastery of San Juan Bautista or Corias. Its construction had the objective of connecting the monastery with the town of Corias. Although its foundations are believed to be of Roman origin, the bridge was rebuilt in 1571, as indicated by an inscription preserved on one of the stones of its parapet.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 44.9 kilometers long and generally takes about 3 hours and 14 minutes to complete by mountain bike. This duration can vary based on your pace and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Coria a Torrejoncillo – circular por Rincón del Obispo trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring a mix of agricultural fields, lush riparian vegetation along the Alagón River, and natural areas. You can expect wide dirt roads, gravel paths, and potentially some paved sections as it connects Coria, Torrejoncillo, and Rincón del Obispo. It offers serene views of the river and its diverse birdlife.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is classified as a 'moderate mountain bike ride' and is considered suitable for all skill levels, though good physical fitness is recommended. The climbs are manageable, and technical sections are not overly challenging.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or points of interest can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting features. The route passes by the historic Iron Bridge of Coria, built between 1901 and 1909, and the Medieval Bridge of Coria. You'll also pass through the 'white colonial town' of Rincón del Obispo and near La Encamisá of Torrejoncillo. The area is also rich in historical and ecological sites, including ancient roads and archaeological deposits.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Coria a Torrejoncillo – circular por Rincón del Obispo trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Coria, being the starting point, offers various parking options within the town. It's advisable to look for public parking near the historic center or the Alagón River, which would provide convenient access to the route.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region of Extremadura are dog-friendly. However, given the route passes through agricultural areas and settlements, it's recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially near livestock or populated zones, to ensure their safety and respect local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Extremadura region can experience hot summers. Therefore, the best times to visit are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Winter can also be pleasant, though some days might be cooler.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Coria a Torrejoncillo – circular por Rincón del Obispo mountain bike trail. It is a public route accessible to all.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will ride along parts of the Camino del Rincón, Camino del Batan de Duque, and various segments of the Pista del Canal. Margen Izquierda, as well as the Camino de la Ermita.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: The route connects the towns of Coria, Torrejoncillo, and Rincón del Obispo. These towns offer opportunities to find cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and get refreshments during your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses areas with diverse birdlife, especially along the Alagón River and around natural springs like "Charco del Moro." The ecosystem also supports various rural species and typical riverside forest fauna, offering opportunities for wildlife spotting.
